title: leontodon hispidus subsp. pseudocrispus
intro: Leontodon hispidus subsp. pseudocrispus, also known as the hairy hawk's beard or rough hawkbit, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family, Asteraceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal medicine, where it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and is used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal issues. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Leontodon hispidus subsp. pseudocrispus can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a qualified healthcare professional before using it as a treatment for any medical condition.
